neighbors.name: Steven J. Brennan & Francesca Brennan
neighbors.claims: Steven J. Brennan is a licensed real estate broker and attorney based in Tucson, Arizona ([Martindale](https://www.martindale.com/attorney/steven-brennan-410873/)). He has a background in law enforcement and is associated with Cambridge Investment Research ([BrokerCheck](https://brokercheck.finra.org/individual/summary/2739189)). Francesca Brennan is the owner of La Mariposa Resort in Tucson ([BBB](https://www.bbb.org/us/az/tucson/profile/health-club/la-mariposa-resort-1286-1305)). The Brennans are non-resident landowners in Cochise County, with their primary professional and residential ties located in Pima County ([Zillow](https://www.zillow.com/homedetails/1632-N-Mariposa-Woods-Pl-309-Tucson-AZ-85749/455799539_zpid/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Non-resident owners; primary influence is in Pima County

approach_recommendations.engage: Approach via formal communication. Given Steven's legal and real estate background, ensure all project documentation is precise and emphasizes property value protection.

neighbors.name: Frank Cullen Brophy
neighbors.claims: In 1935, Frank Cullen Brophy acquired the historic Babacomari Ranch, which remains the largest contiguous private land parcel in Arizona ([Babacomari Ranch History](https://babacomariranch.com/history)). The Brophy family has placed over 3,300 acres of the ranch under conservation easements to protect grasslands and wetlands ([Arizona Land and Water Trust](https://alwt.org/trust-protects-historic-babacomari-ranch/)). In 2024, the family obtained a conditional use permit to develop a guest ranch on 2,200 acres of the property, a project estimated to cost between $40-60 million ([Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/new-40-60-million-dude-ranch-gets-first-permit/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Prominent ranching family; largest private land parcel in AZ

approach_recommendations.engage: Approach through a respected local rancher or conservation contact, emphasizing the project’s environmental stewardship and any legacy benefits (e.g., land conservation measures) to align with the Brophy family’s preservation values.

citations.title: Babacomari Ranch History

citations.title: Trust Protects Historic Babacomari Ranch

citations.claim: In 2024, the family obtained a conditional use permit to develop a guest ranch on 2,200 acres
citations.title: New $40-60 Million Dude Ranch Gets First Permit

neighbors.verification_notes: Added details regarding the 2024 guest ranch permit to demonstrate active development interests. While 'Babacomari Solar North LLC' exists as a developer entity, no direct evidence of the family's stance on *that* specific project was found in the text, though the name implies a relationship.

neighbors.name: State of Arizona
neighbors.claims: This parcel is owned by the State of Arizona, managed as State Trust Land by the Arizona State Land Department ([ASLD](https://land.az.gov/our-agency-mission/history-trust-land)). The Department has a fiduciary obligation to maximize revenue from the trust, often through leasing for uses including renewable energy ([Sonoran Institute](https://sonoraninstitute.org/files/pdf/strategies-for-renewable-energy-projects-on-arizonas-state-trust-lands-01202011.pdf)). As public land, it is not associated with a private resident and has no individual neighbor stance.
neighbors.influence_justification: Public (state) land

approach_recommendations.engage: Not applicable for private outreach—coordinate with the appropriate state agency through formal channels if required.

citations.title: Strategies for Renewable Energy Projects on Arizona's State Trust Lands

neighbors.verification_notes: Confirmed State Trust Land status. ASLD generally leases for solar but has no specific 'neighbor' stance on adjacent private projects.

neighbors.name: JRL Development LLC
neighbors.claims: JRL Development LLC is an Arizona limited liability company with a documented history of land development in Cochise County dating back to at least 2005 ([biologicaldiversity.org](https://biologicaldiversity.org/programs/public_lands/pdfs/20051114-COVEY-RUN-ADEQUATE.pdf)). The company is the recorded owner of parcels 106-47-151 and 106-47-153, which have been identified in local water right adjudications ([superiorcourt.maricopa.gov](http://www.superiorcourt.maricopa.gov/SuperiorCourt/GeneralStreamAdjudication/docs/0245ord061219.pdf)). Unlike a new market entrant, JRL Development LLC has been the developer for the 'Covey Run' subdivision in Hereford, AZ, indicating long-term involvement in local residential development rather than purely speculative land banking ([biologicaldiversity.org](https://biologicaldiversity.org/programs/public_lands/rivers/pdfs/covey-run-PUBLIC-REPORT-20140509.pdf)). Historical public filings have listed Dave Groberg as a contact or member, though current beneficial ownership may not be fully disclosed in all public-facing records ([biologicaldiversity.org](https://biologicaldiversity.org/programs/public_lands/pdfs/20051114-COVEY-RUN-ADEQUATE.pdf)). The entity's activities appear focused on residential subdivision and land holding ([homes.com](https://www.homes.com/property/8665-s-sun-quail-terrace-hereford-az/7fyefe5h8202n/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Established local developer with history of subdivision projects (Covey Run); likely has knowledge of county planning processes but no specific political stance found.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage as a peer developer; acknowledge their history with Covey Run and discuss how the project complements future residential or land value goals.

neighbors.name: Town of Huachuca City (City of Huachuca City)
neighbors.claims: The Town of Huachuca City is the municipal government holding title to parcel 106-47-065 ([parcelinquiry.azurewebsites.us](https://parcelinquiry.azurewebsites.us/)). The Town has explicitly documented its support for renewable energy in its General Plan, specifically Objective 3.5, which states the Town will 'Work to establish some form of alternative energy for the town... and potentially serve as another revenue stream... such as a solar panel array' ([huachucacityaz.gov](https://www.huachucacityaz.gov/Documents/PublicDownloads/Huachuca%20City%20General%20Plan-05152017.pdf)). This objective identifies potential locations for such projects, including reclaimed landfill sites or other town parcels ([huachucacityaz.gov](https://www.huachucacityaz.gov/Documents/PublicDownloads/Huachuca%20City%20General%20Plan-05152017.pdf)). As a municipality, the Town controls local zoning and land-use decisions, making it a critical stakeholder for any nearby development ([huachucacityaz.gov](https://www.huachucacityaz.gov/Documents/PublicDownloads/Huachuca%20City%20General%20Plan-05152017.pdf)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Municipal entity with zoning authority and explicit General Plan objectives supporting renewable energy development.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage directly with Town Council and Planning Department; highlight alignment with General Plan Objective 3.5 and potential for municipal revenue or partnership.

citations.claim: Municipal government holding title to parcel 106-47-065
citations.title: Cochise County Treasurer Parcel Inquiry

citations.claim: General Plan Objective 3.5 states the Town will 'Work to establish some form of alternative energy... such as a solar panel array'

neighbors.name: Military Brothers Moving LLC
neighbors.claims: Military Brothers Moving, LLC is an Arizona moving services company founded in 2014 by two veteran brothers, Kevin M. Hoey and Bryan P. Hoey ([Military Brothers Moving](https://www.militarybrothersmoving.com/)). The company’s base of operations is located at 1301 N Cochise Ave in Huachuca City, Arizona ([FMCSA](https://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov/query.asp?searchtype=ANY&query_type=queryCarrierSnapshot&query_param=USDOT&original_query_param=NAME&query_string=2918139&original_query_string=MILITARY20BROTHERS20MOVING20LLC)). Public records categorize the business as a household and commercial moving provider, with no filings indicating involvement in land development or real estate ventures ([BBB](https://www.bbb.org/us/az/sierra-vista/profile/moving-companies/military-brothers-moving-llc-1286-20087490)). No public statements were found linking this LLC to any stance regarding local development projects.
neighbors.influence_justification: Local service business with no identified political leadership roles or significant land holdings.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage owners Kevin and Bryan Hoey with friendly outreach emphasizing how the development could bring business (e.g. moving/storage needs). Given their limited stake and lack of opposition history, maintain good relations but intensive engagement is likely unnecessary.

neighbors.influence_justification: Private family trust with no identifiable public profile or documented community activity in Cochise County.

neighbors.name: Babacomari Ranch Company
neighbors.claims: Babacomari Ranch Company is a family-owned cattle ranch acquired by Frank Cullen Brophy in 1935 and now held by his descendants ([babacomariranch.com](https://babacomariranch.com/)). It encompasses approximately 28,000 acres across Santa Cruz and Cochise counties, recognized as the largest contiguous private land parcel in Arizona ([babacomariranch.com](https://babacomariranch.com/)). The Brophy family placed 2,488 acres of the ranch under a conservation easement in partnership with Arizona Land and Water Trust to protect its habitat and heritage ([alwt.org](https://alwt.org/babacomari-ranch/)). The family has secured a Conditional Use Permit to develop a guest ranch (dude ranch) on a portion of the property to generate sustainable income ([patagoniaregionaltimes.org](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/new-40-60-million-dude-ranch-gets-first-permit/)). Additionally, the ranch is the site of the planned 160 MW Babacomari Solar project, which is expected to come online in 2026 under an agreement with Tucson Electric Power ([tep.com](https://www.tep.com/data-center-esa/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Largest contiguous private landowner in Arizona (28,000 acres); active partner with Tucson Electric Power for 160MW solar project; recipient of national Environmental Stewardship Award.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage family leadership regarding the integration of the 160 MW solar project with their conservation goals; acknowledge their dual role as conservationists and renewable energy hosts.

neighbors.profile_summary: Historic ranching entity actively diversifying revenue through renewable energy hosting (160 MW solar project) and hospitality development.

neighbors.name: San Ignacio Investments LLC
neighbors.claims: San Ignacio Investments LLC is a land-holding entity; a Florida LLC by this name is listed as inactive ([search.sunbiz.org](https://search.sunbiz.org/Inquiry/CorporationSearch/SearchResults?inquiryType=EntityName&searchTerm=SAN%20IGNACIO%20LLC)). The entity is associated with the 'San Ignacio Ranch' subdivision in Elgin/Sonoita, where parcels are marketed for rural residential use ([landsearch.com](https://www.landsearch.com/properties/elgin-az-85611/4701859)). Real estate listings indicate the entity has subdivided land formerly part of the Babacomari Ranch for sale as residential homesites ([redfin.com](https://www.redfin.com/city/19459/AZ/Tucson/amenity/4+acres)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Speculative land holding entity with no identified local leadership or public engagement on energy issues; focused on residential subdivision sales.

approach_recommendations.engage: Contact via registered agent or real estate listing agents; address potential concerns regarding visual impact on their residential lots for sale.

neighbors.profile_summary: Land investment entity subdividing rural acreage for residential sale; likely sensitive to visual impacts on property values.

neighbors.name: Babacomari Ranch Company LLLP
neighbors.claims: Babacomari Ranch Company LLLP is the legal partnership managing the Babacomari Ranch, the largest continuous privately-held tract in Arizona ([babacomariranch.com](https://babacomariranch.com/)). The ranch was recognized with a national Environmental Stewardship Award in 2008 for sustainable land management ([environmentalstewardship.org](https://www.environmentalstewardship.org/winner-gallery/inductees/babacorami-ranch-co-llp)). The entity is actively participating in renewable energy development; Tucson Electric Power (TEP) lists 'Babacomari Solar' as a 160 MW solar project in Cochise County coming online in 2026 ([tep.com](https://www.tep.com/data-center-esa/)). Additionally, 'Babacomari Solar North LLC' has executed mitigation agreements with the Department of Defense regarding the project ([dodclearinghouse.osd.mil](https://www.dodclearinghouse.osd.mil/Portals/134/Mitigation%20Agreements/Babacomari%20Solar%20Project%20Mitigation%20Agreement.pdf?ver=k2iSolJUqdJl1O-J-eczfQ%3D%3D&timestamp=1712776087551)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Major regional landowner hosting a 160 MW utility-scale solar project; holds significant sway in land use and conservation circles.

approach_recommendations.engage: Coordinate with the Brophy family management; acknowledge their existing partnership with TEP and their established role as a renewable energy host.

neighbors.profile_summary: Primary operating entity for the Babacomari Ranch; actively hosting utility-scale solar development.

overview_summary: Screening of 27 neighboring properties—comprising 16 residents and 11 organizations—reveals three supporters and no documented neutral or opposing stances, with 24 positions currently unknown. Among the four high-influence and two medium-influence stakeholders are the Town of Huachuca City and the Babacomari Ranch, a historic 28,000-acre holding owned by the Brophy family that emphasizes land conservation and ranching heritage. The remaining 21 low-influence neighbors consist of private individuals and speculative investors, indicating a low risk of organized opposition if the project aligns with the area's rural character and municipal interests.
